description:
## Background

`bsm-kia.13` extracts redeclared typed-RPC interfaces, lambdas,
DOM helpers, and inline sidebar selectors from per-spec copies
into two canonical helper modules. Without this expand step the
migrate batch would have to invent the canonical surfaces in the
same commit it switches every consumer, breaking the
expand–contract pattern.

## What to build

Two sibling helper modules under `e2e/issue-list/helpers/` that
own the canonical implementations of every redeclared surface
and every sidebar selector literal.

- `e2e/issue-list/helpers/rpc.ts` — typed-RPC interfaces, the
  `executeAsync` lambdas, and the row/visibility DOM helpers.
- `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` — sidebar selector strings
  and named assertions (`expectCurrentWorkspace`,
  `expectNoCurrentWorkspace`, `expectRememberedWorkspace`).

## Technical Approach

- Create `e2e/issue-list/helpers/rpc.ts` exporting:
  - `WorkspaceSwitchResponse` and `WorkspaceStateResponse`
    re-exported from `src/rpc/bindings.ts` where possible;
    otherwise redeclared with one canonical shape.
  - `invokeTypedWorkspaceSwitch(candidatePath: string)` with a
    canonical `arguments_: object` typed signature.
  - `invokeWorkspaceState()` with the same canonical signature.
  - `issueRowSelector(title: string): string` for the DOM
    selector.
  - `expectIssueVisible(title: string)` that locates the row and
    asserts presence with a clear error message.
- Create `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` exporting:
  - `expectCurrentWorkspace(basename | path)` — assert the
    sidebar shows the expected Current Workspace.
  - `expectNoCurrentWorkspace()` — assert the chooser is empty.
  - `expectRememberedWorkspace(basename)` — assert a remembered
    workspace appears in the chooser list.
- The literal selector string `"[aria-label='Workspace'] ..."`
  lives only inside `helpers/sidebar.ts`.
- Document each helper's contract in a short JSDoc block.
- Do not import either helper from any spec yet — that is the
  migrate sub-issue's job.

## Considerations

- The typed signatures must match the generated TauRPC binding
  types in `src/rpc/bindings.ts`.
- Keep one canonical `arguments_: object` typed signature
  across all callers — no `Record<string, unknown>` drift.
- Keep pure helpers unit-testable (no WebdriverIO dependency in
  `issueRowSelector` if possible — just a string formatter;
  sidebar basename matching if pure).
- The selectors inside the helper modules are the single source
  of truth; future `WorkspaceSelector.tsx` rename or restructure
  touches one helper file, not four spec files.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `e2e/issue-list/helpers/rpc.ts` and
      `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` exist and export the
      canonical types, lambdas, helpers, and sidebar assertions
      listed above.
- [ ] No spec imports either new module yet (this is the expand
      step).
- [ ] The literal selector `[aria-label='Workspace']` (or any
      equivalent) appears only inside `helpers/sidebar.ts`,
      nowhere else.
- [ ] `pnpm exec ultracite check`, `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`, and
      `pnpm test` all pass.
- [ ] Existing `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` and
      `pnpm e2e:issue-list:isolation` still pass with no
      behavioral change.

## Test Plan

- Add Vitest unit test files
  (`e2e/issue-list/helpers/rpc.test.ts` and
  `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.test.ts`) that verify:
  - `issueRowSelector(title)` produces the expected selector
    string for representative titles.
  - Any pure formatter or matcher helper (e.g., title escaping,
    sidebar basename matching) returns the expected value.
- Run `pnpm test`, `pnpm exec ultracite check`, and
  `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` to prove no regression.
title: Expand: create e2e/issue-list/helpers/rpc.ts
